title: Gaude Polonia Scholarship Programme
description: A six-month scholarship of Poland's Minister of Culture and National Heritage for young foreign artists and literary translators (film, photography, art conservation, music, visual arts, theatre, museology, criticism) to develop their craft in Poland under the mentorship of established Polish creators and cultural institutions. Recipients get a monthly gross stipend of PLN 5,000 plus up to PLN 3,000/month toward accommodation. Primarily open to artists from Belarus and Ukraine, with occasional slots for other Central/Eastern European and post-Soviet countries.
org: National Centre for Culture (Narodowe Centrum Kultury) / Polish Ministry of Culture and National Heritage
visaNote: Not an employment position; non-EU nationals (e.g., Belarusian and Ukrainian citizens) need a Polish national (D) visa or residence permit for the 6-month stay.

title: Czech Government Scholarships for Citizens of Developing Countries
description: A Czech government scholarship funding Bachelor's, Master's, and PhD study at Czech public universities for nationals of countries on the Ministry of Foreign Affairs' priority list of developing countries. Covers a monthly stipend (CZK 12,000-13,000, higher for Master's-degree holders), tuition, and a mandatory preparatory year of Czech language instruction. Applications are submitted through the applicant's home-country Czech embassy.
org: Government of the Czech Republic (Ministry of Education, Youth and Sports & Ministry of Foreign Affairs)
visaNote: Non-EU citizens need a Czech long-term study visa/residence permit; open only to citizens of MZV-designated priority developing countries, not to EU or Czech nationals.

title: Hellenic Scholarship Programme for Foreign Students from Developing Countries
description: A Greek state scholarship administered by IKY (State Scholarships Foundation) for nationals of developing countries to pursue Greek-language courses and postgraduate study in Greece. Covers a EUR 600 monthly stipend, free meals and educational materials, a EUR 150 settling-in allowance, EUR 1,000 in arrival/departure travel expenses, and exemption from tuition fees. Applications are submitted through the Greek embassy in the applicant's home country.
visaNote: Non-EU nationals need a Greek student visa/residence permit; open to citizens of IKY-designated developing countries, applied for via the local Greek embassy.

title: Swedish Institute Scholarships for Global Professionals
description: A fully-funded scholarship for citizens of around 34 eligible low- and middle-income countries to pursue a full-time master's degree in Sweden, aimed at professionals with demonstrated leadership experience who intend to contribute to their home country's development. Covers full tuition fees, a monthly living allowance (SEK 12,000), and a one-time travel grant (SEK 15,000, or SEK 10,000 for a specified set of Eastern Partnership countries).
visaNote: Non-EU/EEA citizens need a Swedish residence permit for studies; open only to citizens of Swedish Institute-designated eligible countries (list published annually on si.se).

title: Government of Ireland International Education Scholarships
description: A competitive scholarship from Ireland's Higher Education Authority for high-achieving non-EU/EEA international students admitted to a master's or PhD programme (NFQ Level 9/10) at an eligible Irish higher education institution. Provides a EUR 10,000 stipend plus a full tuition and registration fee waiver for one academic year; around 60 scholarships are awarded annually.
org: Higher Education Authority (HEA), Government of Ireland
visaNote: Open only to applicants domiciled outside the EU/EEA, Switzerland, and the UK; non-EU/EEA nationals need an Irish student visa (Stamp 2) for the academic year.

title: Gabo Fellowship in Cultural Journalism
description: An annual week-long fellowship in Cartagena, Colombia for 15 working journalists (5 Colombian, 5 from elsewhere in Latin America, 5 from other continents) run by Fundación Gabo in partnership with Colombia's Ministry of Culture. Fellows attend writing clinics and mentored field-reporting sessions on a chosen cultural theme, with international economy-class flights, hotel accommodation, and meals covered. The 2026 edition (4th, focused on African culture's influence in the Colombian Caribbean) is open for applications.
visaNote: Most nationalities can enter Colombia visa-free as tourists for the roughly one-week program; check requirements by nationality.

title: Peace Corps Peru - Business Development Advisor
description: A U.S. Peace Corps volunteer assignment in Peru's Community Economic Development sector, supporting local entrepreneurs and small businesses. Covers approximately 3 months of training plus 2 years of service, with round-trip transportation, housing, a monthly living allowance, and full medical/dental coverage provided. Open only to U.S. citizens aged 18+.
visaNote: Peace Corps arranges the necessary Peruvian visa/legal status for Volunteers; open only to U.S. citizens.

title: Charles Darwin Foundation Volunteer Programme, Galapagos
description: An international volunteer programme at the Charles Darwin Research Station in the Galapagos Islands, supporting biodiversity research and conservation projects such as species monitoring, natural history collections, and invasive-species/habitat restoration work. Open to applicants 18+ worldwide (students with 2+ years of undergraduate study or working professionals); a minimum 3-month commitment is recommended. Food and accommodation are typically covered, depending on the project's budget.
visaNote: Foreign volunteers staying over 90 days need an Ecuadorian temporary/volunteer visa plus the mandatory Galapagos transit control card (INGALA); coverage of costs can vary by project budget.
